mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.4pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving sustained corporate revenue expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production crossing below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, forcing equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals disinflationary momentum, prompting immediate equity risk-on flows and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL declines > 0.3pp MoM are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 50% of observed instances over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate, where payrolls rise but unemployment simultaneously increases, indicates labor force participation shocks that suppress earnings growth and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence metric shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple macro cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues, establishing a persistent upward drift in equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates exhibit zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) in a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percentage points month-over-month signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, boosting profit margins and triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO accelerations > 0.5pp MoM fail to generate positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.
